frozen: ground truth annotated manually by the author after reading the chat, BEFORE writing any extractor or running any A/B; never modified afterwards

active_claims.what: the pricing-page A/B test is NOT the dominant cause of the week-4 retention drop (drop appears in both arms: 13.8% test vs 13.1% control)
working hypothesis: the redesigned onboarding caused the drop
organic-search signups (legacy onboarding) showed flat week-4 retention in the same window — a weak but supportive quasi-control
seasonality is a tentative rival hypothesis that cannot be fully excluded with current data

active_constraints.what: do not roll back the onboarding change yet; instrument the funnel first
do not claim externally that onboarding causes the drop until funnel data is available

decisions.what: rule out the pricing change as the primary cause (based on the A/B comparison)
keep the new onboarding live; instrument the funnel; reassess in two weeks
treat the organic-search comparison as a quasi-control, not an airtight control

open_conflicts.what: PM wants a fast answer for next week's review; analysis discipline says wait for funnel data — speed vs rigor

open_questions.what: were there other infra or product changes in that week that could be confounders (check the changelog)
is the drop in the activation funnel itself or in the post-onboarding flow
how do we surface the speed-vs-rigor conflict to leadership rather than paper over it
